rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH KARNAZES VS. SAINT PAUL TRAVELERS INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 12, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANTS TOM COLLINS, HARRY GILBERT, ST. PAUL SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as the fraud causes of action (causes of action 3 through 7). After having been given leave to amend to plead these causes of action with more specificity, including the allegation of facts showing justifiable reliance, Plaintiff has not pled these causes of action with the requisite specificity, and has failed to allege justifiable reliance. Further, the burden is on Plaintiff to show how the pleading can be amended, and she has failed to do so in her opposing papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: HEZZACK DANIELS VS. RECOLOGY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 12, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANTS RECOLOGY INC., and RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court overrules defendant's demurrer to the 10th cause of action for failure to prevent violation of FEHA. The claim fails only where it stands alone, and plaintiffs can pursue this theory of liability if they can establish the underlying wrong, e.g., discrimination. See Trujillo v. N. County Transit Dist., 63 Cal.App.4th 280, 287-89. The Court sustains without leave to amend the demurrer to the 7th cause of action for invasion of privacy as to the Dorton plaintiffs. The invasion of privacy cause of action did not survive decedent's death. See Flynn v. Highman (1983) 149 Cal.App.3d 677, 683. The Court overrules the demurrer to the 4th and 14th causes of action as to the Dorton plaintiffs because it appears that these causes of action are not based solely upon the invasion of privacy. A party cannot demur to part of a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RONDA A. RIGAMONTI VS. LIBERTY MUTUAL FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Requests For Production And Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 12, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANT LIBERTY MUTUAL FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Requests For Production And Special Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin , a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to address how the court can determine whether or not the second settlement agreement contains words to the effect that it is enforceable or binding that would bring it within the purview of Evidence Code Section 1123(b). Liberty Mutual is entitled to discovery of information sufficient to allow it to determine what proportion of the attorney fees Ms. Rigamonti incurred were for defense of the action she claims Liberty Mutual had a duty to defend; what proportion of the fees were incurred in prosecution of her against Mr. Gragnani; and also to determine what amounts Ms. Rigamonti actually paid her attorneys. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to present their positions on what is the minimum additional evidence that should be produced, if any, in order to provide Liberty Mutual with that information. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: SAMI YOUSSEF DDS VS. DENTAL BOARD OF CALFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administritve Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 12, 2012, Line 26, PETITIONER SAMI YOUSSEF'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandate. Denied. Petitioner was not denied a fair trial. Each sentence of the charge is not a separate element that must be proven. There is no indication in the record that Respondent improperly shifted the burden to Petitioner. The administrative law judge did not commit a prejudicial abuse of discretion. The administrative law judge found that Petitioner misrepresented AB's portion of the cost of her dental care "himself, or through his staff¿" (AR00035). There was sufficient evidence to show that Petitioner either made the representations himself or had knowledge of and ratified the representations regarding payment. Respondent did not need to establish fraud in order to prevail on its charge. Respondent was not required to provide expert testimony to support the charge. The Decision is supported by the findings, and the findings are supported by the evidence. Upon independent review, the Court agrees with the administrative law judge's finding that Petitioner's testimony about the amount billed to AB and statements made to AB regarding this amount was confusing and inconsistent. Respondent's objections are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 12, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 6, AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin , a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Plaintiff has waived all objections by failing to timely respond to the discovery propounded. Within 15 days of entry of this order Plaintiff shall serve on Defendant verified code compliant responses to Defendant's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One. Sanctions are DENIED. The declaration of counsel does not sufficiently segregate the hours actually spent on preparing the motion from the time Defendant's counsel anticipates spending in appearing at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DIANE LINEHAN VS. BAXTER INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Answers And Documents From Session One Of Pltf'S Deposition; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 12, 2012, Line 27, Motion To Compel Further Answers And Documents From Session One Of Plaintiff's Deposition; Request For Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Watson as follows: Denied. The motion is time-barred. Plaintiff waited 88 days after reporter notified parties that transcript was ready to make her first attempt to meet and confer regarding deposition questions that Plaintiff had not answered. Further, on the merits, the motion is denied. Discovery sought is not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, and/or is irrelevant. The request for production of documents is denied; the requested item was not properly sought under the CCP. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND SARNO VS. SEARIVER MARITIME INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Deposition Of The Plaintiff And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 12, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of The Plaintiff And Pos Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin , a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff shall appear for one additional hour of deposition on a mutually agreeable date in January 2013 at the offices of Sterling & Clack in San Francisco, California. The subject matter of further questions shall be limited to exploring plaintiff's mental and emotional state at or about the time he became aware that the Coast Guard was involved in the casualty, and his emotional reaction to the Coast Guard's charges. This order is without prejudice to any party's ability to argue at trial that the evidence is or is not admissible. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: JOE DRUMMOND et al VS. FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Directing Compliance With Subpoena And For Expenses Of The Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 12, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 7, JOE DRUMMOND, JOYCE REITMAN, RICHARD PERRILLO, KEITH WHITEMAN'S Motion For Order Directing Compliance With Subpoena And For Expenses Of The Motion Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin , a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiffs already have the General Ledger for 2009, and it need not be produced again. The General Ledgers for 2008, 2010, 2011 and 2012 shall be produced to counsel for plaintiffs. The parties shall enter into a stipulated protective order that allows a party to designate information as "confidential." Defendant may designate the General Ledgers produced as "confidential" pursuant to the protective order. Names of any party to this litigation or any person or entity who is not a homeowner appearing in the general ledgers shall not be redacted. Names of any homeowner who is not a party to this litigation and their unit number (if it appears in a general ledger entry ) shall be redacted and replaced by a unique identifier for each homeowner (e.g., homeowner 1, homeowner 2, etc.) such that the identity of the homeowner is protected while still allowing plaintiff's counsel to determine all of the ledger entries attributable to a particular, anonymous homeowner. Entries in the "notes / remarks" field of the general ledgers shall not be redacted. Plaintiff shall pay copying costs of 15 cents per page. Plaintiffs shall also pay redacting costs of $10 per hour actually spent in redacting the general ledgers, not to exceed a maximum of $200 in redacting costs per ledger. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
